chain.thought: Explanation: The youngest age at the start date (when each became the oldest living president) is 49 years, 241 days for Theodore Roosevelt, making him the youngest.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
The table lists each instance of a president becoming the “oldest living president” along with their age at the start date. To find the “youngest living president” in this context, we identify the minimum “age at start date.” Theodore Roosevelt became the oldest living president at 49 years, 241 days (row 14), which is younger than all others (e.g., Taft at 51 years, 170 days; Grant at 53 years, 95 days; all others older). Therefore, Theodore Roosevelt is the youngest to hold the status.
